Event: Escalates a minor slight during a collection job into attempted murder
Explanation: His petty escalation brings no gain and gets him and Chris lost and near death.

Event: Attempts to murder Valery and chases him into the woods
Explanation: Tries to remove a perceived threat but mainly creates danger for all involved.

Event: Gets himself and Christopher hopelessly lost in the woods during the botched hit
Explanation: Their exposure and near-death stem directly from his incompetence and stubbornness.

Event: Shoos the cat out of Satriale's back room
Explanation: Mildly asserts his comfort while slightly inconveniencing the animal.

Event: Initially defends Vito against rumors and demands proof
Explanation: Protects a colleague from unproven accusations and calms the situation.

Event: Turns on Vito and pushes for killing him after confirmation
Explanation: Supports eliminating Vito to satisfy mob norms and his own outrage while gravely harming Vito.

Event: Questions Tony’s leadership over the handling of Vito
Explanation: His challenge risks his standing and stability without clear benefit to anyone.

Event: Talks at extreme length on the phone until Beansie falls asleep
Explanation: Indulges himself socially while imposing on Beansie’s time and patience.

Event: Stands with others confronting Pussy about being an informant
Explanation: Helps preserve the crew’s security at the cost of Pussy’s safety and life.

Event: Expresses envy and hostility toward colleagues praised by Tony
Explanation: His posturing protects his status but poisons relationships and group cohesion.

Event: Refuses promotion to Aprile crew captain until threatened with Patsy getting it
Explanation: Reluctantly accepts a powerful role to protect his own position and the crew’s order.

Event: Reconciles with Nucci after initially disowning her
Explanation: Regaining their bond benefits his emotional life and gives her comfort and support.

Event: Shows Papa Bear tendencies toward Little Paulie
Explanation: Protecting his nephew strengthens family bonds and his own support network.

Event: Objects to Furio attacking a child
Explanation: Opposing the attack preserves some moral boundary and reduces harm to a vulnerable kid.

Event: Helps get Tony off Georgie when Tony is beating him
Explanation: De-escalates Tony’s violence, slightly safeguarding himself and sparing Georgie further harm.

Event: Participates in executing Pussy in cold blood
Explanation: Eliminates a dangerous informant for family security at the cost of Pussy’s life.

Event: Shoplifts mugs from the Starbucks expy as “payback”
Explanation: Gains stolen goods and petty satisfaction while harming the business.

Event: Acts like an entitled owner in Valery’s house while collecting
Explanation: Throws his weight around to assert power, provoking resentment and later violence.

Event: Comforts his mistress’s young children after waking them with a night terror
Explanation: Reassures frightened kids while preserving his relationship with their mother.

Event: Comforts Beansie after Richie cripples him
Explanation: Shows concern that supports Beansie emotionally and maintains his civilian goodwill.

Event: Assures Finn he’ll suffer no retribution after testifying about Vito
Explanation: Reduces Finn’s fear and keeps him cooperative, which helps the family.

Event: Shares rumors and secrets, including implying Adriana was blowing Tony
Explanation: Gets social capital as a gossip while endangering reputations and internal trust.

Event: Gives Johnny Sack inside information used to advantage New York
Explanation: Seeks favor with New York but weakens his own family’s position.

Event: Believes Johnny Sack’s flattery about Carmine’s interest and keeps feeding info
Explanation: He gains little real benefit while empowering New York at his own family’s expense.

Event: Tells Christopher newly made men should take problems to Tony
Explanation: Good procedural advice increases order and reduces impulsive violence.

Event: Gets outraged when others go to Tony with problems about him
Explanation: Tries to insulate himself from accountability, maintaining power while harming fairness.

Event: Tells Johnny about Ralph’s fat joke after scolding Little Paulie for repeating it
Explanation: Uses the joke to curry favor and hurt a rival, sparking dangerous NY tension.

Event: Expecting special treatment after his prison stint despite resenting others for it
Explanation: Leverages his time inside for status and perks while breeding resentment and unfairness.

Event: Demands a sit-down to grab more of the first heist’s spoils from Ralph
Explanation: Attempts to maximize his take at the cost of fairness and inter-crew relations.

Event: On a later heist, hoards as much of the take as he can using arguments he once opposed
Explanation: Profits by contradicting his previous stance, damaging trust and cooperation.

Event: Disowns Nucci after learning she isn’t his biological mother
Explanation: He inflicts emotional harm on both of them and destabilizes his only real family.

Event: Forgives Nucci and cares for her until she dies
Explanation: Restoring and maintaining the relationship benefits his psyche and her final years.

Event: Shows concern and some grief over Christopher’s death and regrets mistreating him
Explanation: His remorse and reflection promote better self-understanding and honor their bond.

Event: Beats and extorts an innocent man out of jealousy over his loving birth mother
Explanation: Gets money and emotional venting while severely harming an innocent victim.

Event: Murders Minnie Matrone after she catches him robbing her
Explanation: He secures money and covers his crime at the cost of killing an elderly woman.

Event: Inflates a restaurant bill to punish Christopher for complaining
Explanation: He gains petty revenge and a free meal while undermining trust with his subordinate.

Event: Murders a seizing waiter instead of getting help after Chris throws a brick
Explanation: He prioritizes avoiding implication over the waiter’s life, gaining only risk reduction.

Event: Is openly a “complete dick” to Valery while collecting, triggering the fiasco
Explanation: His needless cruelty provokes chaos that nearly kills him and Chris.

Event: Judges and mocks the intervention mediator for being a “two-time loser”
Explanation: He boosts his own ego while damaging the mediator’s authority and the process.

Event: Mocks Christopher’s joy over his newborn daughter becoming an “idiot stripper”
Explanation: He asserts dominance and cynicism, straining a fragile family relationship.

Event: Cuts safety corners at an amusement fair he manages, causing an accident
Explanation: Saves money and increases his skim while endangering and injuring civilians.

Event: Tells Johnny Sack about Ralph’s Ginny Sack joke, sparking NY tensions
Explanation: Gets attention from Johnny at the expense of major, lasting conflict.

Event: Starts schmoozing New York behind Tony’s back
Explanation: He attempts to hedge his bets personally while undermining his boss’s position.

Event: Turns on Vito “out for blood” once the truth comes out
Explanation: Pursues harsh mob justice that satisfies him but destroys Vito.

Event: Attempts to defect to New York and is humiliated when Carmine barely knows him
Explanation: His betrayal backfires, hurting his standing while offering almost nothing to others.

Event: Denies to Tony that he was the one feeding Johnny information
Explanation: Protects his own life while keeping Tony in the dark and resentful.

Event: Organizes and participates in an intervention for Christopher
Explanation: Despite his behavior, backing intervention aims to help Chris and stabilize the crew.

Event: Shows visible grief at Nucci’s funeral
Explanation: Authentic mourning aids his own coping and honors the person who raised him.

Event: Regrets mocking Christopher’s sobriety after Chris dies
Explanation: His regret leads to self-awareness and some emotional growth.

Event: Destroys Christopher and Kelli’s garden with his car after Little Paulie is beaten
Explanation: His retaliatory rampage vents anger but needlessly damages family property and ties.

Event: Sniffs Adriana’s panties during a late-night inspection visit
Explanation: Gains perverse gratification while violating her privacy and trust.

Event: Refuses to apologize to Christopher for sniffing Adriana’s panties
Explanation: Preserves his pride and dominance while deepening resentment.

Event: Acts as an overbearing mentor to Christopher, yet still teaches him the ropes
Explanation: His guidance improves Christopher’s competence and reinforces Paulie’s status.

Event: Helps clear a room of Colombian drug dealers in his late 60s
Explanation: Strengthens his crew’s criminal position by killing rivals or enemies.

Event: Serves as manager of an amusement fair to skim and control revenue
Explanation: Gains money and influence but degrades safety and business integrity.

Event: Spends significant income providing for his mother
Explanation: His financial support benefits both her well-being and his sense of duty.

Event: Keeps his health, diet, and fitness disciplined compared to other mobsters
Explanation: Improves his longevity and reliability, indirectly helping the crew.

Event: Feeds Johnny Sack information that he thinks will secure a better position for himself
Explanation: Attempts personal advancement while weakening his own side’s leverage.

Event: Accepts promotion as Tony’s successor under veiled threat of Patsy getting it
Explanation: Taking the job preserves his power and ensures some continuity for the family.

Event: Talks behind Tony’s back during periods of discontent
Explanation: Vents and seeks allies while eroding loyalty and organizational cohesion.

Event: Remains utterly loyal to the Mafia lifestyle and refuses to work with Feds
Explanation: Protects his standing and safety inside the mob while perpetuating criminal harm.

Event: Openly mocks and belittles troublemakers like Richie, Ralph, Vito, Feech, and Chris
Explanation: His hostility sometimes protects him but also escalates conflicts and animosity.

Event: Survives by staying paranoid and wary of almost everyone
Explanation: His hyper-vigilance keeps him alive and incidentally steers him away from some threats.

Event: Kills elderly Minnie Matrone after she discovers his robbery
Explanation: Gains loot and covers his crime through lethal violence against a vulnerable victim.
